mechanism_narrative: A sustained 3-month decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A monthly decline in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) above 100k signals weakening labor demand, prompting central banks to delay rate hikes and driving S&P 500 forward returns positively over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5-day returns in 60%+ of instances where PAYEMS declines exceed 100k, or predictive power vanishes in out-of-sample tests (p > 0.1).

mechanism_narrative: A 20-day rise in industrial production (INDPRO) above its 50-day moving average indicates strengthening manufacturing activity, boosting corporate earnings and generating positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC drops below 0.05 in 3-year rolling windows or 20-day returns fail to outperform baseline in 50%+ of cases (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A concurrent decline in residential construction (HOUST) and rising consumer prices (CPIAUCSL) signals stagflationary pressures, causing equity markets to discount future earnings and generate negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX shows positive 60-day returns in 70%+ of cases where both HOUST declines and CPI surges occur, or interaction effect size falls below 0.1 (p > 0.1).

mechanism_narrative: A 1-month rise in the money supply (RSAFS) above its 12-month peak indicates liquidity-driven asset price inflation, creating positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days through increased investor risk appetite.
failure_condition: SPX fails to outperform baseline in 50%+ of instances despite RSAFS surges, or predictive power collapses in 3-year rolling windows (IC < 0.03).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month decline in unemployment rate (UNRATE) below 5% signals tight labor markets, prompting wage inflation and central bank rate hikes that drive negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX shows positive 60-day returns in 60%+ of cases where UNRATE falls below 5%, or predictive power vanishes in out-of-sample tests (p > 0.1).

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous drop in GDP (GDPC1) and rising housing starts (HOUST) indicates economic contraction with mixed signals, creating negative S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days due to heightened uncertainty.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 declines > 1% QoQ and HOUST rises < 2% MoM
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in 60%+ of cases where GDPC1 declines and HOUST rises, or interaction effect size falls below 0.1 (p > 0.1).

mechanism_narrative: A 20-day rise in industrial production (INDPRO) above its 200-day moving average combined with falling oil prices (not in catalog) would indicate strong supply-side growth, but since oil isn't available, we use INDPRO alone to predict positive SPX returns over 20 days.
failure_condition: Rank IC drops below 0.05 in 3-year rolling windows or 20-day returns fail to outperform baseline in 50%+ of cases (p > 0.05).
